shortcut: Main clause = SVO like English. Subclause = verb kicks to the END.
explanation: Dutch is SVO in main clauses but SOV in subordinate clauses. This trips everyone up.

patterns.rule: Yes/No question: flip S and V
Subclause (omdat, dat, als...): verb goes LAST
Two verbs in subclause: infinitive comes last

memory: 🧠 Subclause = function call — you stack verbs at the end like arguments: subject + [modal] + [infinitive].

examples.nl: Ik ga naar huis omdat ik moe ben.
examples.en: I'm going home because I'm tired.

examples.nl: Hij zegt dat hij niet kan komen.
examples.en: He says that he can't come.

practice.nl: Ik ga naar huis omdat ik moe ben.
practice.en: I'm going home because I'm tired.

practice.nl: Ik leer Nederlands omdat ik in België woon.
practice.en: I'm learning Dutch because I live in Belgium.
